content:
Hi @Trotini! I notice that none of your test cases hit the upper boundary of your constraint: 3 ≤ N ≤ 10. Do you think you could add/modify some test cases to hit that upper boundary of 10, or adjust that boundary to match your test cases? Additionally, the longest player name in all of your test cases is 10, but your 2nd constraint says 1 ≤ length of player name ≤ 20. Could you adjust the constraint or add/modify some test cases to hit that upper boundary?

Other than that, I think this is a really good concept for a clash, and I'm ready to approve once you have made those changes :)

Output
* The order of lines DOES matter, as the referee can only compare the players' output with the expected output you've set. Please replace "The order of lines does not matter" with the required order.

Constraints
* Please change 100 to 10, as [[N]] never exceeds 10.

Thanks.

You're right, that was from the first version when you thought you could add validation code for the problems, but then you couldn't. Thanks for letting me know, and the 100 doesn't matter, right? The complexity of this problem is always O(n).

Output
* I'd asked to replace "the order of lines does not matter" with the required order, but it was removed instead. Please explicitly state the correct order as players need the instruction.

Constraints
* On the change from 100 to 10, you're right that it doesn't affect the complexity here. That said, it can still matter in a couple of ways:

(a) In Shortest Mode, someone might choose to allocate a fixed-size array upfront, and smaller bounds can influence the number of characters used.

(b) More generally, it's good practice to keep constraints aligned with the actual limits of the problem. There will be scenarios where that precision really matters.

Minor note
> when you thought you could add validation code for the problems, but then you couldn't.
* The "you" references should be "I", since you were referring to your own earlier approach.

Recap
* Please add the correct order in the Output section.

Thanks.

Thanks again for writing and giving me recommendations.
I'll change that now, and regarding what you said in the "minor note," I apologize for my mistake. I'm Spanish, and English is a bit difficult for me, so I write my texts in Spanish and run them through a translator. I skim those texts because I have a basic understanding, but I didn't notice that basic error.

No worries at all! 🙂

Output
* Could you please replace "example" with "players as given in the input"?

Thank you.
